source_jobs.title: Staff Backend Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR



 - We’re a global e-invoicing platform operating in 25+ countries.

 - Revenue grew 10x last year, and we plan to 3x in 2026.

 - Role: own integrations end-to-end, research, define, build, ship, and iterate with customers.

 - Looking for: 10+ years of backend experience, strong product judgment, fluent English, builder mentality, comfort with ambiguity, and complex domains.

 - Compensation: €90k–€140k base + Stock Options.

 - Location: Madrid (at least 3-4 days/month in our office, travel expenses paid).



⸻



Hi there 👋🏼,



I’m Juan, the CEO of Invopop. We help companies like Fever, DeepL, Booksy, and SumUp issue and receive invoices that comply with local requirements across 25+ countries.



We’re looking for a Staff Backend Product Engineer to join our team. If you enjoy solving hard problems with elegant software, owning your roadmap, and helping shape what we build (and how), this role could be a great fit.




ABOUT INVOPOP



Invopop helps global companies like SumUp, Fever, and DeepL issue and receive invoices across more than 25 countries through a single API.



In the next five years, more than 40 countries, including the entire EU, will mandate that businesses send invoices electronically (in XML format) and report them in real-time to tax authorities. This reduces fraud and enables companies to automate invoice processing instead of relying on OCR. There’s only one catch: each country has defined unique standards, forcing global companies to build dozens of local integrations—or integrate once with Invopop. :)



Invopop was founded by Sam Lown, co-founder and former CTO of Cabify, based on his experience dealing with this problem at Cabify.



We’ve raised €2.8M from top-tier global VC firms, including YCombinator (W23), and have been named the Best Startup at South Summit 2024 and Madrid's Hottest Startup of 2024 by Wired.




YOUR MISSION AS A STAFF BACKEND PRODUCT ENGINEER



With Invopop, users build workflows by chaining Actions, each action being managed by an App. An App is a standalone service focused on a single goal, such as reporting an invoice to a tax authority, synchronizing it with an external system (e.g., ERP), or transforming/enriching a document.



As a Staff Backend Product Engineer, you’ll be the owner of several Apps end-to-end, from the initial research to maintaining in production:



 - Investigate new countries and systems: read regulations and technical docs, turn ambiguity into clear requirements, and consult local experts when needed.

 - Design and build integrations in Go on top of our modular architecture (NATS, CQRS patterns, Kubernetes on GCP).

 - Drive product decisions: define what “good” looks like, choose trade-offs, cut scope when needed, and ship.

 - Support customers using your Apps and iterate based on real feedback.

 - Raise the bar across the team: review PRs, mentor engineers, and influence product and technical direction (architecture, patterns, quality




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR



 - 10+ years building backend systems (APIs, distributed systems, or complex domains). Golang is a plus, but not required.

 - Fluent English: it's our internal working language.

 - Strong product judgment: you can scope, prioritize features, and make trade-offs under ambiguity.

 - Customer empathy: you’re comfortable speaking with customers and translating feedback into product decisions.

 - A track record of high-agency: you’ve led projects end-to-end, improved systems over time, and made teams faster through reviews, standards, and mentorship.




COMPENSATION



€90k–€140k base + Stock Options, depending on seniority level.




WHERE YOU’LL WORK



We’re based in Madrid (Calle Guadalquivir, El Viso), 5 minutes from Nuevos Ministerios.



We genuinely enjoy working in the office, and right now the team benefits significantly from high-context, fast decision-making in person. So our default is Madrid-based.



That said, for a truly exceptional engineer who can ope

source_jobs.title: Founding Marketer
source_jobs.description:
QUICK FACTS

 - We're a global e-invoicing platform operating in 30+ countries

 - Revenue grew 10x last year and is on track to triple again this year to €3M ARR

 - Role: first marketing hire. Build the content and inbound engine from scratch alongside our VP of Compliance and product team, turning complex regulation into content finance leaders actually want to read

 - Looking for: 2+ years in B2B SaaS marketing or startups, exceptional English writing skills, strong taste, and curiosity about how AI can improve your craft

 - Compensation: €60–80k + ESOP

 - Based in Spain (remote, with monthly visits to our Madrid office)

If this sounds like you, keep reading.



⸻

Hi there—I’m Juan, CEO of Invopop. 👋🏼



Over the last few years, we’ve built a product customers genuinely love, along with strong outbound and partner sales motions. Now it’s time to build inbound!



In our market, inbound matters enormously. Companies start looking for an e-invoicing provider when regulations suddenly become urgent, and they make decisions fast. Being the company they discover — and instantly trust — is critical for us.



I’m looking for a Founding Marketer to build that engine with me.



We work in a deeply technical and regulated space, so great marketing here requires curiosity, precision, and taste. You should treat writing as a craft and use AI to augment it, not replace it. You should enjoy spending days understanding a hairy topic before explaining it simply. And you should know the difference between content that fills space and content that earns trust.



You’ll work closely with our VP of Compliance, product team, and me to turn complex regulation into the clearest, sharpest content in the industry. You’ll also own other parts of marketing, from paid acquisition to webinars and events.



You’ll report directly to me, build the marketing function from scratch, and, over time, grow into the Head of Marketing as you build the team around you.





ABOUT INVOPOP

Invopop helps global companies like Qonto, Hyundai, and DeepL issue and receive invoices across more than 30 countries through a single API.



Over the next few years, more than 50 countries — including the entire EU — will mandate electronic invoicing and real-time reporting to tax authorities.



The opportunity is massive, but the problem is painful: every country has its own formats, APIs, workflows, and edge cases. Most global companies would need dozens of local integrations to stay compliant. Or one integration with Invopop.



Invopop was founded by Sam Lown, co-founder and former CTO of Cabify, after dealing with this problem firsthand, together with Juan Moliner (ex-BCG).



We’ve raised €2.8M from top-tier global investors, including Y Combinator (W23), and were named Best Startup at South Summit 2024 and Madrid’s Hottest Startup by Wired.




YOUR MISSION AS FOUNDING MARKETER

You'll build the marketing engine that makes Invopop the obvious choice when a Finance, Tax, or Tech leader starts evaluating e-invoicing providers.



What you'll do:

 - Own the content engine end-to-end: research, write, edit, publish, and distribute. Blog posts, LinkedIn content, webinars, customer stories, white papers, and country guides. The goal is simple: produce the best content in the industry.

 - Work closely with our VP of Compliance and product team. They’ll bring the regulatory depth; you’ll turn it into clear, useful content for finance, compliance, and engineering audiences.

 - Build the inbound funnel: SEO, landing pages, lifecycle emails, attribution, and conversion paths. Create the foundations for future scale.

 - Run paid marketing efforts across channels like Google and LinkedIn. Own strategy, execution, and experimentation.

 - Help shape adjacent surfaces when needed: positioning, website copy, events, webinars, and newsletters.

 - Use AI thoughtfully. We expect you to already be experimenting with tools that improve research, drafting, localization, or d

source_jobs.title: Founding Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QUICK FACTS

 - We’re a global e-invoicing platform operating in 30+ countries

 - Revenue grew 10x last year and is on track to triple again this year to €3M ARR

 - Role: first dedicated post-sales hire. Own customer implementations end-to-end and build the post-sales function from scratch alongside the founders and engineering team

 - Looking for: 2+ years of experience in technical, customer-facing, or implementation roles. Strong coding fundamentals, clear communication, and high ownership

 - Compensation: €50–70k + ESOP

 - Based in Madrid, working in person from our office in Madrid



--

Over the last few years, we’ve built a product customers genuinely love. More than 200 companies have implemented Invopop using only our API docs and support channels.



As we move upmarket, larger enterprises need more hands-on technical support to design, build, and launch their integrations successfully. That’s why I’m looking for a Founding Solutions Engineer to help us build the post-sales function from scratch.



You’ll work directly with engineering teams at some of the world’s fastest-moving tech companies to get them live across multiple countries. You’ll debug integrations, answer hard technical questions, design rollout plans, and help customers move from signed contract to production launch.



You should be technical enough to read logs, reason about integration architecture, and write quick scripts when needed — but also pragmatic, organized, and comfortable working directly with customers.



You’ll report directly to me initially and, over time, help shape how implementations and technical onboarding work at Invopop.




ABOUT INVOPOP

Invopop helps global companies like Qonto, Hyundai, and DeepL issue and receive invoices across more than 30 countries through a single API.



In the next five years, more than 40 countries, including the entire EU, will mandate that businesses send invoices electronically in XML format and report them in real time to tax authorities. This reduces fraud and enables companies to automate invoice processing instead of relying on OCR. There’s only one catch: each country has defined unique standards, forcing global companies to build dozens of local integrations—or integrate once with Invopop. :)

 

Invopop was founded by Sam Lown, co-founder and former CTO of Cabify, and Juan Moliner (ex-BCG), based on Sam’s experience dealing with this problem at Cabify

 

We’ve raised €2.8M from top-tier venture capital firms, including YCombinator (W23), and have been named the Best Startup at South Summit 2024 and Madrid's Hottest Startup of 2024 by Wired.




YOUR MISSION AS FOUNDING SOLUTIONS ENGINEER

Your job is simple: help customers launch successfully with Invopop.

This includes:

 - Owning Enterprise customer onboarding from contract signature to production launch

 - Working directly with customer engineering teams to design and debug integrations

 - Help customers think through rollout plans and implementation approaches

 - Answer technical questions during the sales process alongside the commercial team

 - Improve API docs and build ERP connectors based on your learning

 - Build the playbook for implementations and technical onboarding




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-have:

 - 2+ years of experience as software engineer or post-sales technical support

 - Excellent written and spoken English

 - Comfortable reading API docs, logs, HTTP requests, and JSON/XML payloads

 - Able to write code or scripts when needed

 - Strong communication, ownership, and structured thinking

 - Comfortable working directly with customers and driving projects forward

Nice to have:

 - Familiarity with fintech, ERP, billing, tax, or compliance systems

 - Spanish




WHERE YOU'LL WORK

We’re in Madrid (Calle Guadalquivir, El Viso), 5 minutes from Nuevos Ministerios.

No fixed schedule. We expect at least 3 days/week in the office — high-context work moves faster in person. Sam and I are often in the

source_jobs.title: Backend Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR



 - We’re a global e-invoicing platform operating in 25+ countries.

 - Revenue grew 10x last year, and we plan to 3x in 2026.

 - Role: own integrations end-to-end — research, define, build, ship, and iterate with customers.

 - Looking for: 1–10 years of backend experience, builder mentality, fluent English, comfort with ambiguity, and complex domains.

 - Compensation: €50k–€90k base + Stock Options.

 - Location: Madrid (at least 3 days/week in our beautiful office in El Viso, Madrid).



⸻



Hi there 👋🏼,



I’m Juan, the CEO of Invopop. We help companies like Fever, DeepL, Booksy, and SumUp issue and receive invoices that comply with local requirements across 25+ countries.



We're looking for a Backend Product Engineer to join our team in Madrid. If you enjoy solving challenging problems with elegant software solutions, owning your roadmap, and directly interacting with customers, this role could be a great fit!




ABOUT INVOPOP



Invopop helps global companies like SumUp, Fever, and DeepL issue and receive invoices across more than 25 countries through a single API.



In the next five years, more than 40 countries, including the entire EU, will mandate that businesses send invoices electronically in XML format and report them in real time to tax authorities. This reduces fraud and enables companies to automate invoice processing instead of relying on OCR. There’s only one catch: each country has defined unique standards, forcing global companies to build dozens of local integrations—or integrate once with Invopop. :)



Invopop was founded by Sam Lown, co-founder and former CTO of Cabify, and Juan Moliner (ex-BCG), based on Sam’s experience dealing with this problem at Cabify



We’ve raised €2.8M from top-tier venture capital firms, including YCombinator (W23), and have been named the Best Startup at South Summit 2024 and Madrid's Hottest Startup of 2024 by Wired.




YOUR MISSION AS A BACKEND PRODUCT ENGINEER



With Invopop, users build workflows by chaining Actions, each action being managed by an App. An App is a standalone service focused on a single goal, such as reporting an invoice to a tax authority, synchronizing it with an external system (e.g., ERP), or transforming/enriching a document.



As a Staff Backend Product Engineer, you’ll be the owner of several Apps end-to-end, from the initial research to launching, and maintaining them in production:



 - Investigate new countries and systems: read regulations and technical docs, turn ambiguity into clear requirements, and consult local experts when needed.

 - Design and build integrations in Go on top of our modular architecture (NATS, CQRS patterns, Kubernetes on GCP).

 - Make product decisions: what and how to build it, what to cut, and when to ship.

 - Support customers using your Apps and iterate based on real feedback.

 - Contribute to GOBL, our open-source standard for commercial documents.




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR



 - Backend engineering experience, ideally with Golang.

 - Strong communication skills in English.

 - Empathy for customers—you're eager to engage with clients and influence product decisions.

 - Comfortable defining the scope as you build, learning new technologies, and navigating unfamiliar domains.

 - Experience starting projects from scratch—perhaps as a founder, open-source contributor, or through impactful side projects.




COMPENSATION



€50k–€90k base + Stock Options, depending on seniority level.



If you have 10+ years of experience and are looking for a higher-leverage role (broader scope, leading multiple tracks), we’re also hiring for a Senior Product Backend Product Engineer (€90k–€140k base + ESOP).




WHERE YOU’LL WORK



We’re in Madrid (Calle Guadalquivir, El Viso), 5 minutes from Nuevos Ministerios.

source_jobs.title: Graduate Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR



 - We're a global e-invoicing platform operating in 25+ countries

 - Revenue grew 10x last year, and we plan to 3x in 2026.

 - Role: own integrations end-to-end, research, define, build, ship, iterate with customers. No PMs or bureaucracy in between.

 - Looking for: new grads engineers with strong software development fundamentals, fluent English (no Spanish required), a builder mentality, and comfort with ambiguity.

 - Compensation: €45k base + Employee Stock Options (ESOP)

 - Location: Madrid (4-5 days/week in office).



⸻



Hi there 👋🏼,



I’m Juan, CEO of Invopop. We help companies like Fever, DeepL, and Booksy issue and receive invoices that comply with local requirements across 25+ countries.



We hire only exceptional early-career talent. If you’re looking for the fastest way to become a great engineer, or eventually start your own company, it’s hard to beat working directly with Sam Lown (founding CTO of Cabify) and Juan Moliner (ex-BCG) and a small, high-bar team where your work ships to production and direct customer interaction.



This is a full-time role for someone who has completed (or is about to complete) university and can join Invopop within 1–2 months.




ABOUT INVOPOP



Invopop helps global companies like SumUp, Fever, or DeepL manage electronic invoicing in over 25 countries through a single API.



In the next five years, more than 40 countries, including the entire EU, will mandate that businesses send invoices electronically in XML formats and report them live to tax authorities. This reduces fraud and enables companies to automate invoice processing instead of relying on OCR. There’s only one catch: each country has defined unique standards, forcing global companies to build dozens of local integrations—or integrate once with Invopop.



Invopop was founded by Sam Lown, co-founder and former CTO of Cabify (Spain's first tech unicorn), and Juan Moliner (ex-BCG), based on Sam’s experience dealing with this problem in ten Latin American countries with Cabify



We’ve raised €2.8M from top-tier venture capital firms, including YCombinator (W23), and have been named the Best Startup at South Summit 2024 and Madrid's Hottest Startup of 2024 by Wired.




YOUR MISSION AS A GRADUATE PRODUCT ENGINEER



You’ll own one or more Apps from research to production

 - Design and build backend integrations in Golang

 - Investigate new countries and systems: read docs, interpret requirements, ask questions, and turn ambiguity into a clear plan.

 - Make product decisions with support: what to build first, what to cut, when to ship.

 - Support customers using your integrations and iterate based on real feedback.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR



 - Degree in Computer Science or Telecommunications Engineering, or a proven track record building software outside formal education.

 - Evidence of exceptional ability: top of your class, standout projects, or similar

 - Builder mindset: side projects, open-source contributions, hackathons

 - Fluent English (spoken and written). It’s our working language.

 - You can join full-time in Madrid within the next 1-2 months




WHERE YOU'LL WORK



We’re in Madrid (Calle Guadalquivir 22, El Viso), 5 minutes from Nuevos Ministerios.



We enjoy the energy and collaboration of working together in person. We expect you to default to working in-person, but trust you to find the best balance between office and remote work.




WHY JOIN INVOPOP



 - Real ownership from day one: you’ll ship production work that customers rely on.

 - Steep learning curve: you’ll grow fast with direct feedback and high standards.

 - Work closely with Sam Lown (founding CTO of Cabify) and a small, senior team.

 - Hyper-growth: 15 launches planned in 2026 and a 3x growth goal.

 - High-signal culture: good product and good engineering win.




INTERVIEW PROCESS



The process has four steps:

 - Intro call (30 min)

 - Take-home exercise

 - Technical interview (1h)

 - Final interview,

notes: High-growth e-invoicing platform with strong engineering focus
